lessons.title: Element Not Visible — Hidden by CSS or Not Yet Loaded
lessons.context: Elements can be hidden in multiple ways: display:none, visibility:hidden, opacity:0, or not yet added to the DOM. The fix depends on the cause — CSS-hidden elements need a trigger action, while not-yet-loaded elements need a wait.

problem.error: Element not visible / hidden / display: none
problem.cause: Element hidden by CSS (conditional rendering) or not yet added to DOM (async load)

antipattern.description: Asserting visibility without taking the action that triggers the element to appear
antipattern.why_bad: Element is hidden because a prerequisite action wasn't taken — waiting alone won't make it appear
code: // BAD — element is hidden because form wasn't submitted

solution.description: Identify why the element is hidden and take the appropriate action before asserting visibility.
code: // GOOD — trigger the action that makes element visible
await expect(page.getByText('Success! Your order has been placed.')).toBeVisible();
// GOOD — wait for async element to appear

lessons.ai_instruction: When asserting element visibility, first identify what triggers the element to appear (button click, API response, tab switch). Generate the trigger action before the visibility assertion.

lessons.title: Element Is Disabled — Fill Required Fields Before Clicking
lessons.context: Submit buttons are often disabled until all required form fields are filled. Clicking a disabled button has no effect in Playwright — it doesn't throw an error, it just does nothing. This causes tests to silently fail at the next assertion.

problem.error: Element is disabled / button click has no effect
problem.cause: Button is disabled because required form fields are empty or validation has not passed

antipattern.description: Clicking a button without filling required fields first
antipattern.why_bad: Clicking a disabled button silently does nothing — the next assertion fails with a confusing error
code: // BAD — button is disabled, click has no effect

solution.description: Fill all required fields and verify the button is enabled before clicking.
code: // GOOD — fill required fields, verify enabled, then click

lessons.ai_instruction: Before generating a button click step, check if the button has enable conditions (required fields, checkboxes). Generate all prerequisite fill steps first, then add toBeEnabled() assertion before the click.
